title: FOI2025/08916: Contracts
body: "   Reference number FOI2025/08916   Date of request 9 September 2025   Date of response 3 October 2025   Outcome Fully withheld - Section 12 cost limit     The Department for Culture, Media and Sport (the department) received the following request for information which we responded to under the Freedom of Information Act 2000 (FOIA):  “I wish to see full copies of all of current and former legal agreements you hold such as:    Access agreements.   Assistance agreements.   Memorandums of understanding.   Contracts/subcontracts.   Partnership agreements.    With the following companies:    Melody Ai Limited   TheLogically Limited   Kreatur Limited   Crisp Thinking Group Limited   Crisp Thinking (UK) Limited   Crisp Thinking (US) Inc   Palantir Technologies Inc.   Palantir Technologies UK, Ltd   Anthropic PBC   Anthropic Limited   Disinformation Index Ltd   Faculty Science Ltd   Behavioural Insights Ltd   Institute for Strategic Dialogue”    Our response  We have dealt with your request under the Freedom of Information Act 2000 (the Act). We regret that we are unable to respond to your request because it exceeds the cost limit set out by the Act. Section 12 of the Act makes provision for public authorities to refuse requests for information where the cost of dealing with them would exceed the appropriate limit, which for central government is set at £600. This represents the estimated cost of one person spending 3.5 working days in determining whether the department holds the information, locating, retrieving and extracting the information.  We consider your request would be over the statutory limit because at presently worded, the request is too broad as it asks the department to check each individual contract and supplier listed.  In order that you can submit a request that does not breach the cost limit set, you may like to significantly reduce the amount of suppliers/external stakeholders you would like us to contact in any future requests (i.e. 2 or 3).  Depending on the information you wish to receive you may like to consider refining your request using the above mentioned suggestion.  If you would like further information regarding the cost limit and how it is applied please refer to the Freedom of Information and Data Protection (Appropriate Limit and Fees) Regulations 2004. "
